meaning: vt.鍗峰叆,鍚炴病,鐙煎悶铏庡捊
examples: The crisis threatens to engulf several others.
I have come into the deep waters; the floods engulf me.
The next Ice Age could take only weeks to engulf Britain.
It takes the colony 20 minutes to complete the pass, while their deafening chirps engulf the night sky.
It threatens to engulf Italy, severely testing the bloc's financial ability to handle the crisis.
